title: Bridge Essay: Conflicted between Goal-Directed and Habitual Control, an fMRI Investigation.
concept: Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.
**Phase context:** Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

**Conflicted between Goal-Directed and Habitual Control, an fMRI Investigation.** — eNeuro (2018). DOI: 10.1523/ENEURO.0240-18.2018. PMID: 30310863. "Slips of action" occur in everyday life when we momentarily lose sight of a goal (for example, when in a rush or distracted). Associative models propose that these habitual responses can be activated via a direct stimulus-response (S-R) mechanism, regardless of the current hedonic value of the outcome.
